cards.title: RAIN AT RESCUE ROW
cards.body:
The rain started three days ago and hasn't stopped.

The yard is filling up, and the animals need cleared area before the water reaches them.

cards.title: TAP TWO OR MORE
cards.body:
Tap any connected group of 2 or more matching supplies to clear it.

The water only rises when you move, so look as long as you need.

cards.title: RISING WATER LEVELS
cards.body:
The light blue shallow water shows where it will flood next and rises each turn.

Flooded rows are already lost.

cards.title: CLEAR THE WAY
cards.body: Clear tiles in all four directions around Daisy.

cards.title: DAISY
cards.body:
Mae found her under the supply shelf the night the rain started. Small, scruffy, watches everything.

She hasn't moved from that corner since. She's waiting for someone to clear the way.

cards.title: Crates Need a Neighbor Clear
cards.body:
Crates don’t break when you tap the crate.

Clear supplies beside them. The hit comes from the neighboring clear.

cards.title: CLEAR HER FOUR SIDES
cards.body:
When the path above, below, left, and right of Daisy is open, she can reach safety.

Corners don't count. Just the four direct sides.

cards.title: THE SORTING DOCK
cards.body:
Cleared supplies get put in the dock. Mae sorts them when she has three of the same kind.

Seven spaces. That's all the room she has.

cards.title: THREES SORT AWAY
cards.body:
Three matching supplies sort themselves out of the dock automatically.

Everything else stays until you match it. If the dock fills completely, there's nowhere left to put things, and the rescue stops.

cards.title: MULTIPLE RESCUES
cards.body:
Two animals need help. The water is closer to one of them.

Mae can't reach both at the same time. Someone has to go first.

cards.title: THE ONE IN DANGER GOES FIRST
cards.body:
Save the animal closest to the water before it reaches them.

If you clear the safe one first, you might run out of moves before you get back to the one who needed you more.

cards.title: FROZEN OVER
cards.body:
The temperature dropped last night. Some of the supplies in the yard are frozen solid.

Mae can't move them until the ice breaks, but sometimes she can see what's underneath.

cards.title: BREAK THE ICE
cards.body:
Clear a group next to a frozen tile and the ice cracks open. The supply inside joins the board.

Sometimes the fastest rescue path is locked under the frost.

cards.title: WILD VINE
cards.body:
The rain brought the vine back. It grows fast in wet ground, and it grows toward safe space.

The paths you clear for the animals? That's exactly where the vine wants to go.

cards.title: CUT IT BACK
cards.body:
Clear a group next to vine to remove it. But if you leave vine alone too long, it grows into the open tiles you already cleared.

Watch the tendrils. When they're reaching, you're running out of time to cut them.

cards.title: STILL STANDING
cards.body:
That was the hardest one yet. Everything at once: water, vine, ice, and two animals counting on you.

You handled it.
